chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. B.A. No.630 of 2026 (Kamran Khan vs. The State) For hearing of bail application Date of hearing & order 31.03.2026 Mr. Tajjamul H. Lodhi, advocate for applicant Ms. Firdous Faridi, Special Prosecutor, Custom a/w IO Asif Jamal, Preventive Officer, Custom O R D E R ----- Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J:- Applicant is seeking post arrest bail in a case bearing Crime No.P-33/2025-IMO, u/s 6/9 8, 14 & 15 of CNS Act, 1997 (as amended in 2022), registered at Police Station Custom, Karachi, by means of this application. 2. On 05.12.2025 at 11:00 hours, at International Mail Office, ICG Shed, Jinnah International Airport Customs, Karachi, a suspected parcel was received, which on examination was found containing 9455 MDMA tablets weighing 3.980 kilograms, hence, an FIR was registered. The case against the applicant is that on 19.12.2025, he came to the said office and inquired about the impugned parcel, hence he was arrested. In the investigation, it was disclosed by him that he had come to inquire about the parcel on the direction of his friend, namely, Naeem Khan, who was found in contact with the main accused Sher Muhammad found to be the owner of the parcel in the investigation. 3. Learned counsel for the applicant has pleaded for bail on the ground that the possibility of the applicant being an innocent inquirer cannot be ruled out, as during the investigation, except, the fact that he came to ask about the parcel, no evidence has been found connecting him with the alleged parcel. 4. Learned Special Prosecutor, Custom has opposed the bail. The Investigating Officer, however, has admitted that except the fact that applicant made an inquiry about the parcel, no further evidence has been collected against him. 5.

Learned Special Prosecutor, Custom has opposed the bail. The Investigating Officer, however, has admitted that except the fact that applicant made an inquiry about the parcel, no further evidence has been collected against him. 5. We are of the view that in the given circumstances it is yet to be established by the prosecution as to whether the applicant was in knowledge of the contents of the parcel, or he was just used by his friend co-accused Naeem to make an inquiry about the arrival of the parcel. Accordingly, bail application is allowed and the applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.200,000/ (Rupees two hundred thousand 2 only) with P.R. bond in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 6. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. JUDGE JUDGE Rafiq/PA
